channel.title: The Grant Desk - Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, next 30 days.

item.title: British Council Ireland: UK-IRL Culture Connects Grant 2026/27
item.description:
Organisation: British Council Ireland / Arts Council Ireland

Location: Ireland and the UK. For bilateral partnerships of one individual based in Ireland and one based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales; in-person collaboration required.

Award: Up to EUR 3,000 per collaboration (smaller amounts also eligible), covering artist fees (including your own time), collaborator fees, travel and accommodation, studio or space hire, materials, and access costs including disability support. No application fee.

Duration: Activity between October 2026 and March 2027; must be completed by 31 March 2027

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The UK-IRL Culture Connects Grant 2026/27 is a British Council Ireland initiative delivered in association with Arts Council Ireland, supporting the early stages of artistic collaboration between Ireland and the four UK nations through in-person exchange. It prioritises creative development over final outputs: research and development of new ideas, mentorship, exploring collaborative pathways with producers or partners, experimenting with new technologies or practices, and developing initiatives that support creative exchange. WHO: applications are made by individual artists or creative practitioners working as a bilateral partnership of one individual based in Ireland and one based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales; applicants must be resident in Ireland or the UK for the project duration, have a proven professional track record, be at an early or developing career stage, and not be in full-time education. One application per collaboration. FUNDING: up to EUR 3,000 covering artist and collaborator fees, travel and accommodation, studio hire, materials, and access costs. Activities must involve in-person collaboration (fully remote projects are not eligible) and take place between October 2026 and 31 March 2027. Apply via the British Council GoodGrants platform; an information webinar recording and overview document are on the programme page. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OUTsider Fest 2027: Dreaming US into Being (call for queer time-based art)
item.description:
Organisation: OUTsider

Location: Austin, Texas, United States (festival February 2027). International-friendly; prioritizes marginalized artists including by migratory status and nationality.

Award: Festival presentation slot for time-based work (film, performance, sound, multimedia). Artist compensation and travel support not stated. No fee stated.

Duration: Festival February 2027.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

OUTsider, the transdisciplinary queer arts festival in Austin, invites LGBTQ+ artists working in any time-based form (film, performance, sound, multimedia) to propose work for its 2027 edition themed Dreaming US into Being. The call prioritizes marginalized artists, explicitly including by migratory status and nationality. Deadline 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Global Artivism: Governance Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,200 to 1,800 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting a Governance Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on governance systems, democratic institutional design, and Global Majority-led movement infrastructure. The contractor supports the development and maintenance of Global Artivism's governance architecture, including documentation, committee coordination, and research that supports the Democratic Transition process, and is expected to take initiative on assigned tasks and grow through structured mentorship. The movement is governed by a Global Council with a Movement Secretariat coordination team; the Fellow coordinates primarily with the Governance Lead alongside the Operations Director, Secretariat members, and Council committees (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: governance documentation (frameworks, committee charters, terms of reference, process documentation; maintaining an organized, current, consistently formatted document library; preparing materials for Global Council meetings); committee coordination (scheduling, materials, meeting notes, follow-up, trackers of decisions and action items, agendas and briefing packs); Democratic Transition and electoral process support (research on governance models, electoral processes, and democratic participation mechanisms; drafting materials for the Democratic Transition Roadmap 2025-2027; preparing electoral framework and testing documentation; tracking milestones); and governance research (comparative analyses and briefing notes; staying current on distributed governance, participatory democracy, and decentralized leadership; contributing to knowledge products). REQUIRED: manages multiple documentation tasks and meets deadlines with attention to accuracy and formatting (experience via union organizing, participatory facilitation, cooperative governance, or community-based decision-making counts alongside academic or formal work history); good written English; ability to take initiative and work independently; genuine interest in governance, movement building, or institutional design; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in governance, law, political science, or international development; interest in Global Majority leadership and distributed power; civil society/NGO/movement experience; familiarity with arts, culture, or social justice movements; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Global Artivism: Operations Fellow
item.description:
Organisation: Global Artivism (a fiscally sponsored project of Res Publica US Inc.)

Location: Remote, open globally. Priority given to applicants from the Global South.

Award: USD 1,000 to 1,500 per month (independent contractor fee), paid on receipt of monthly invoices. No employee benefits, paid leave, or insurance; contractor covers their own taxes and compliance.

Duration: 12-month independent contractor engagement from August 2026, with potential renewal subject to funding and mutual agreement. Flexible hours with scheduled calls across time zones.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Global Artivism, a Global Majority-led movement that engages the power of arts and culture toward a just world on a safe planet, is contracting an Operations Fellow for a mentored, time-limited engagement focused on organizational infrastructure and systems design. Working alongside the Operations Director, the contractor designs operational frameworks spanning financial workflows, contractor management, governance alignment, and onboarding, contributing directly to infrastructure decisions while building practical experience in how a Global Majority-led movement organization functions operationally. The Fellow coordinates primarily with the Operations Director alongside the Governance Lead, Project Manager, Admin Coordinator, and Secretariat members (coordination relationships do not constitute supervisory authority; decision-making is distributed through governance structures and committees). KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: infrastructure design and framework development (designing and documenting operational frameworks; contributing to staffing models, organizational structures, and resource frameworks that enable both centralized and decentralized work; designing onboarding and operational guidance; aligning operational systems with governance frameworks and democratic decision-making); systems and knowledge documentation (operational guidance and toolkits for distributed teams; internal knowledge systems such as documentation, checklists, and decision trees); and capacity building and research (identifying where documentation or systems redesign enables teams to work more independently; flagging operational bottlenecks and proposing solutions; sharing learning with Council, committees, and teams; researching operational models relevant to Global Artivism's structure). REQUIRED: manages multiple tasks and meets deadlines with attention to detail and comfort coordinating logistics across a distributed team (experience via coordinating community events, grassroots logistics, or operations in any context counts alongside formal work history); good written English; comfort with digital tools (Google Workspace, Slack, or similar); genuine interest in operations, organizational systems, or movement infrastructure; ability to operate as an independent contractor; and commitment to Global Artivism's values. PREFERRED: background in business administration, project management, or finance; interest in international civil society, movement building, or Global Majority leadership; civil society/NGO/community experience; familiarity with digital project management or operations tools; multilingual ability in languages relevant to Global South contexts. This is an independent contractor engagement managed through Global Artivism's fiscal sponsorship with Res Publica US Inc.; the contractor sets their own hours and methods, may serve other clients, and handles their own taxes and local compliance. TO APPLY: submit an expression of interest via the ABM Intelligence application page; submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis by ABM's consulting team. Expressions of interest close Thursday 16 July 2026, 10pm MYT (UTC+8); late submissions will not be reviewed. Priority is given to candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities, and community organizing, grassroots coordination, and self-taught practice are valued alongside formal credentials.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Connections Through Culture Grants 2026 (British Council)
item.description:
Organisation: British Council

Location: International collaboration grant. Each application must involve at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they apply from throughout the project.

Award: Grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000. Free to apply.

Duration: Project-based; develop and deliver collaborative projects across a range of themes and art forms.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Connections Through Culture (CTC) grants are designed to enable meaningful partnerships, cross-cultural exchange and long-term collaboration between the UK and participating countries. In 2026, CTC incorporates the grants programmes formerly known as Biennials Connect (Visual Arts) and Architecture, Design and Fashion Biennials and Festivals Grants, and serves as the British Council's major Arts grants programme for 2026 (in place of International Collaboration Grants). It offers grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000 to support creative practitioners, cultural professionals, artists, organisations, festivals, biennials, hubs and collectives to develop and deliver collaborative projects across a wide range of themes and art forms, including applied arts, architecture, curating, dance, design, digital, drawing, education, fashion, film, installation, interdisciplinary work, painting, performance, photography, printmaking, public art, research, sculpture, social practice, sound art, textiles, video, visual arts and writing. ELIGIBILITY: applications must involve collaboration between at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they are applying from at the time of application and throughout the project activity period. PARTICIPATING COUNTRIES: Albania, Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Colombia, Mainland China, Egypt, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kosovo, Lebanon, Malaysia, Mexico, Montenegro, New Zealand, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, South Africa, Tanzania, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, UK, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, and Vietnam. Eligible applicants include artists and creative practitioners, arts and cultural organisations, festivals and biennials, cultural professionals, creative hubs/networks/collectives, and organisations working across arts, culture and the creative sectors. Note: applications involving projects organised by or taking place as part of Visual Arts, Architecture, Design, Fashion and Craft festivals should be made by the festival or biennial organisation rather than by individual artists, designers or practitioners. No application fee. Deadline 12 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Steps for Individuals 2026 (Arts Council of Wales)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Wales

Location: Wales, United Kingdom. For artists and creatives based in Wales.

Award: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs). Applicants can apply for up to 90% of the developmental cost; the remaining 10% must come from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both).

Duration: Supports a stage of professional development; allow a minimum of 10 working weeks between the deadline and the project start date.

Deadline: 29 Jul 2026

Creative Steps funds the professional, business and organisational development of artists who have experienced barriers to careers within the arts. Creative Steps for Individuals is open to individual artists and creatives who identify as being Ethnically and Culturally diverse, D/deaf, disabled or neurodivergent, groups that are underrepresented in Arts Council of Wales funding programmes due to barriers to entry. Applicants must have demonstrable experience as an artist or creative professional; those without may still be supported on the basis of artistic potential by submitting a portfolio, CV or examples of work, discussed with a Relationship Manager. WHAT IT SUPPORTS: career development, NOT creating new work or project delivery. Examples include peer-to-peer learning, training courses (not formal/vocational education), building networks, mentorship/coaching, business development/social media/marketing advice, website development/marketing materials/equipment or materials, and setting up a new organisation. Budgets may include the applicant's own time, freelancer costs (paid at standard industry rates), mentor/consultant costs, access costs, and childcare/caregiving costs. AMOUNT: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs); up to 90% of the developmental cost, with the remaining 10% from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both, confirmed by letters of support). No application fee. APPLY: applicants must express interest at least 3 weeks before the deadline to receive support from an Application Coach or Access Support coordinator; an application form is provided by a Relationship Manager. The fund runs in rounds; the next round (Round 2 2026) has an Expression of Interest cut-off of 8 July 2026 and an application deadline of 29 July 2026. Later rounds: Round 3 (EOI 30 September, deadline 21 October 2026) and Round 4 (EOI 10 February, deadline 3 March 2027).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Project Funding for Individual Artists 2026 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Project-based activity; duration not specified.

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

Project Funding for Individuals, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds project-based creative activities across all artistic disciplines. Eligible activities include creating new work, research and development, residencies, mentoring, professional development, and time dedicated to specific creative outcomes. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, artist fees/time, residency costs, travel (mileage at GBP 0.257/mile), venue hire, technical equipment (max 50% of request value), and IT equipment (laptops/PCs up to GBP 1,200; tablets up to GBP 500). ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines and career stages, including emerging artists (less than 5 years professional experience), and in some cases technical staff/administrators; students and academics at third-level institutions may apply with departmental approval showing the costs are not covered by their employer or academic work. AMOUNT: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. No application fee. Deadline: Thursday 30 July 2026, 12:00 noon (online submission required).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Artists' International Development Fund 2026 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland; international development takes place abroad with overseas partners/hosts.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Early-stage international development opportunities; residencies supported for a maximum of 4 weeks.

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Artists' International Development Fund (AIDF), part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds early-stage international development opportunities for individual, freelance and self-employed artists to build networks abroad. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, travel, residencies (up to 4 weeks), technical fees, venue hire, and equipment, with IT devices capped at GBP 1,200 for laptops/PCs and GBP 500 for tablets. ELIGIBILITY: artists across all disciplines, particularly emerging artists (under 5 years professional experience); academics and students at third-level institutions may apply if the project differs from their employment/studies. AIDF applicants must have received recognition for their work in Northern Ireland, lack extensive international experience, and demonstrate interest in their work from an overseas partner/host. AMOUNT: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. No application fee. Deadline: Thursday 30 July 2026, 12:00 noon.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Major Individual Artists Award 2026/27 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland.

Award: Up to GBP 15,000 per award; four awards available in 2026/27. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Supports specific projects, specialised research, personal artistic development and materials/equipment. Activity duration not specified.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

The Major Individual Artists award, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds extended or ambitious work by established artists, covering specific projects, specialised research, personal artistic development, and materials/equipment. AMOUNT: four awards of up to GBP 15,000 each in 2026/27. ELIGIBILITY: established artists across all disciplines with national/international recognition who have not previously received a major award; academics and postgraduate artists are eligible if the project costs do not relate to their institutional work or studies. Applicants must contact the relevant artform officer before submission (by 10 July 2026); applications without this prior contact are ineligible. No application fee. The online application system closes precisely at the deadline and applicants must receive a confirmation email to verify successful submission. Deadline: Monday 20 July 2026, 12:00 noon.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Kometa Fellows 2026: Field Journalism, Photography, and Writing
item.description:
Organisation: Kometa (French literary and journalism magazine)

Location: Remote / field-based (international)

Award: €10,000 per Fellow, six Fellows selected. No application fee.

Duration: One year of funding for field work and research

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Kometa, a French literary and journalism magazine, awards fellowships to journalists, writers, and photographers documenting reality in contexts of war, censorship, or severe social and political tensions. Projects must be rooted in direct field experience and focused on contemporary rather than historical events. Projects already under a contractual agreement are ineligible. AMOUNT: €10,000 per Fellow, six Fellows selected. WHAT FELLOWS RECEIVE: one year of funding; publication of the completed project (partial or full) in Kometa; opportunity to contribute to the monthly newsletter; mentorship through editorial collaboration. 2026 FOCUS AREAS: disinformation and narrative wars; political and social changes in the Gulf. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Tally form (https://tally.so/r/J9XxMK), in French or English (about 30 to 45 minutes). Contact: fellows@kometarevue.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Revitalizing Rituals 2026 Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: Homo Faber Alapítvány (Homo Faber Foundation)

Location: Bhar Lazreg, Tunisia and Somogy County, Hungary; multi-stage in-person residency across both sites.

Award: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Multi-stage residency: Placemaking Camp (1 week, November 2026, Hungary); Storytellers Camp (1 week, March 2027, Tunisia); Residency Period (minimum 1 month, April-July 2027); public presentation (August 2027); Closing Event (3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary).

Deadline: 19 Jul 2026

Revitalizing Rituals invites artists from all disciplines -- sound, movement, visual arts, installation, performance, and transdisciplinary practices -- to join a research-creation journey across two landscapes: Bhar Lazreg in Tunisia and the wetlands of Somogy County in Hungary. The project explores rituals as living forms of belonging: reconnecting communities, land, memory, food, ecological reciprocity, and the more-than-human world. Selected artists work with local communities as co-participants in a shared inquiry into moving from extraction toward reciprocity. Organised by Homo Faber Alapítvány (Hungary). AMOUNT: EUR 6,000 gross artist fee and production budget; international travel and per diems covered; on-site accommodation provided. ELIGIBILITY: minimum 5 years of active artistic practice; portfolio reflecting interest in situated or relational work; no formal degree required; working language is English (local language navigation valued); must be available for the full residency cycle including all international travel; must be able to legally receive the fee and invoice the organising partners from country of residence. Strongly encourages applications from historically marginalised or underrepresented voices. KEY DATES: Application Deadline 19 July 2026 (midnight) -- note the open call PDF also references a 5 July date; the officially promoted deadline is 19 July; Online Q&A 29 June 2026, 14:00 Tunisia / 15:00 CET (register at https://forms.gle/brnZvXvFe4JB8zL26); Online Conversations with shortlisted artists: mid July to mid August 2026; Selection Results: 31 August 2026; Placemaking Camp: 1 week, end of November 2026, Hungary; Storytellers Camp: 1 week, March 2027, Tunisia; Residency Period: minimum 1 month (splittable), April to July 2027; Final Presentation: August 2027; Closing Event: 3 days, winter/early spring 2028, Hungary. Apply via the open call form linked at https://homofaber.hu/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Artist-Open-Call-FINAL-in-the-RR-form.pdf.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Jamverse Jam (Protocolized worldbuilding contest)
item.description:
Organisation: Protocolized (Summer of Protocols)

Location: Online / Worldwide

Award: One USD 1,000 grand prize plus at least 10 additional USD 200 prizes; winning artifacts are published on the Jamverse website. No entry fee.

Duration: One-off contest; submit up to three artifacts by 31 July 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Protocolized's fourth contest invites artists and writers (storytellers, poets, comic artists) to build, extend, and connect the Jamverse, a shared near-future science fiction universe of four intersecting story cycles (The Stockton Chronicles, Legends and Ledgers, UET-Trainverse, and Zoothesia). ELIGIBILITY: open to anyone; you may submit up to three in-universe worldbuilding artifacts, and the same creator can win multiple prizes. REQUIREMENTS: each artifact must be under 1,000 words (200-700 is the sweet spot) or a visual equivalent (comic panel, poster, advertisement, etc.) and must include at least one element drawn from one of the four sub-world 'toy boxes', a surprising 'traffic jam' (an emergent, ah-ha consequence of a technology or protocol), and a relation to the theme of Stigmergy (indirect coordination through modifying a shared environment). Artifact types can include comic panels, job postings, poems, forum threads, historic documents, or encyclopedia entries. APPLY: submit each artifact as a public Google Doc via the contest form, including the artifact, a one-paragraph explanation of the worlds/toy-box components used and how the piece relates to stigmergy and explores a traffic jam, and, if AI was used, documentation of your AI/LLM protocol. PRIZES: one USD 1,000 grand prize and at least 10 USD 200 prizes, all published on the Jamverse website. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. Contest rules, the current Jamverse stories, and starter materials are on the Jamverse website.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Restless Egg: Batch 2, Luxury Technology (Accelerator for Artist-Founders) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Restless Egg

Location: London-based incubator (UK); open to creative "artist-founders" internationally, with in-person gatherings (a first-batch salon was held at Sybil in Berlin).

Award: Equity-based investment (NOT a non-dilutive grant): USD 100,000 initial investment for 5% equity, plus up to USD 175,000 in follow-on investment when predefined performance metrics are met. Includes a high-touch accelerator program and access to a network of subject-matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors.

Duration: Six-month accelerator program

Deadline: 17 Jul 2026

Restless Egg is a London-based incubator for "artist-founders", an emerging genre of creative people whose work sits between art, technology and building commercial products. It invests in creative founders and runs a six-month, high-touch accelerator program to help them build commercially successful and scalable companies at the bleeding edge of culture and technology. BATCH 2 (LUXURY TECHNOLOGY) is open for applications, seeking founders building luxury technologies: commercial products that use AI and other emerging technologies to unlock richer, stranger, more meaningful forms of human experience, not just more efficiency. For this batch Restless Egg has doubled its initial investment to USD 100,000 for 5% equity, and increased its follow-on investment to up to USD 175,000, aiming to give founders more runway, more support and more room to build the most ambitious companies. The program supports rapid learning, sharper product and narrative development, and real investability through close engagement with Restless Egg and its network of subject-matter experts, collaborators, operators and investors; the follow-on is released when predefined performance metrics are met. NOTE: this is a dilutive equity investment (it takes a 5% stake), not a non-dilutive grant or fellowship. Applications are open (no fixed deadline stated). Apply at https://apply.restlessegg.com.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: EU-Japan Fest: Mobility Support 2026 (15 August 2026 Cut-Off)
item.description:
Organisation: EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee (in partnership with Culture Next)

Location: Travel between Japan and European Capitals of Culture / Culture Next member cities

Award: JPY 50,000 to JPY 150,000 per applicant, depending on applicant and travel category. No application fee.

Duration: Travel period April 2026 - September 2026 (applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to travel)

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

The EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee supports individuals and organisations developing projects between Japan and European Capitals of Culture (past, present or future) as well as other Culture Next member cities. PURPOSE: support travel for research, project planning, and collaboration, building creative networks aimed at realising Japan-Europe arts and culture projects. THEMES (for Japan-Europe collaborative projects): Youth Empowerment; Nature & Green; Diversity (DEI); Social Regeneration. Projects may include participants from other regions (e.g. Asia) joining a Japan-Europe project. TARGET APPLICANTS: members of European Capital of Culture teams; artists and project organisers (including curators, designers, etc.); researchers in culture/arts; social entrepreneurs. TIMING: applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to the travel date; this cut-off covers travel in April 2026 - September 2026. DEADLINE: this card is for the 15 August 2026 cut-off (other cut-offs each have their own card). APPLY via the Culture Next collaboration page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
